displayTitle: After the Display: Record, Alarm, Number
kicker: ET LEVEL I // RECORDING, ALARMS & READOUTS

beats.narration: A meter or impedance trace lives only while you watch it. The instant the probe moves on, the signal is gone.

payload.title: AFTER THE DISPLAY
payload.sub: The signal vanishes — unless you record, alarm, or read it.

beats.narration: Three problems remain. The signal vanishes, so you record it. Nobody can watch a production line, so alarms decide. Some jobs want a number.

payload.heading: Three things to do with a signal

beats.narration: Alarms get smart with gates. Rotate the display so lift-off lies horizontal, then drop a sector gate off that axis. Wobble skates by; cracks climb in.

payload.heading: A gate that alarms by phase
payload.note: LIFT-OFF HORIZONTAL · GATE CATCHES CRACK-DIRECTION SIGNALS

beats.narration: Recording follows arithmetic, not pens. Digitizing rate divided by probe speed sets samples per inch, and calibrations bracket every run. A failed closing check means re-examine.

chips.s: Re-examine since the last good one

beats.narration: A number removes interpretation, not discipline. Wrong standard, wrong temperature, or a too-thin part gives a confident, wrong reading. Prove the setup first.

payload.sub: Conductivity, coating, wall — trustworthy only with calibration behind them.
